mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending expectations, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint negative signal fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of rapid credit expansion, rising consumer credit balances amplify discretionary spending, boosting corporate revenues and generating positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth periods consistently produce flat or negative 5-day forward returns, breaking the credit-spending transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth accelerates beyond 3.0%, it signals strong aggregate demand that lifts corporate profit margins, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 3.0 percent
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly higher during GDP growth spikes above 3.0% compared to baseline periods (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and persistent inflation erodes real wage growth, triggering a consumption slowdown that negatively impacts equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and high CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and robust order books, which directly supports forward earnings estimates and yields positive equity returns over a week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month changes and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 900 thousand units signals a sharp pullback in business and consumer confidence, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months where HOUST drops below 900 thousand units.
